compiled_state.author: reconciler

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Planning complete after interactive refinement. Chosen MVP: compact closed creation_channel values cli/mcp/python/jira/import/unknown; explicit producer attribution for new CREATE events; deterministic three-signal Jira-only inference for legacy records with creation_channel_inferred=true; all other legacy records unknown. Actor, environment, source_*, mutation provenance, filtering, and security attestation remain out of scope. Decomposed into 6fe2 (local facades), e622 (Jira/import + inference), and 568c (SNAPSHOT durability), with sequential depends_on links because the reducer/docs surfaces overlap. File impacts and DD verification commands are recorded on the epic and every child.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review round 1: signed PASS, 0 blocking, 9 advisories. Dispositions: ACCEPT F1 (split closure/code-presence proofs into deterministic commands); ACCEPT P5 (added direct 568c -> 6fe2 ordering edge for shared reducer/docs files); ACCEPT P6 (split parent ACs into independently proved outcomes); ACCEPT G6 (specified _ensure_inbound_assignee_identity -> ensure_identity_for -> create_identity_core -> create_core channel threading); ACCEPT T8 (formal JSON Schema const:true marker rule); ACCEPT G4 (assigned identity.py signature ownership to 6fe2 and Jira-boundary reuse to e622); ACCEPT T4 (expanded rollback to retain reader/schema or require consumer notification before full downgrade); ACCEPT G3/G4 (assigned test_export_ndjson.py to e622); ACCEPT G3 (assigned docs/output-schemas.md import semantics to e622). File impacts and verify commands were updated accordingly. Re-running review because these material edits stale round 1 attestation.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan review round 2 disposition (all five advisories accepted and remediated): (1) aligned the legacy inference acceptance-test selector with the planned pytest name; (2) defined the exact shared LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR and LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID constants, their values, location, producer use, and reducer use; (3) specified the SNAPSHOT strip guard as a contract test over _snapshot_strip_keys() with an explicit disjointness assertion; (4) clarified rollback/recompaction behavior: generic compiled-state restore plus the established strip policy preserves unknown provenance keys across immediate pre-feature recompaction, so rollback retains reader/schema support where possible; and (5) assigned SNAPSHOT output parity documentation and its schema-coverage/lint proof to child 568c. File-impact and verification metadata now match those edits.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan review round 3 disposition (signed PASS, 0 blocking, 5 surfaced advisories): accepted the first-compaction rollback hazard and added the exact three-state post-rollback compiled_state matrix, DATA LOSS RISK notice, supported back-out, and full-downgrade compaction/event-retirement precondition to the epic and SNAPSHOT child; rejected the pytest-selector advisory because the current parent AC2 and e622 AC1 both already use the identical -k inbound_recorded selector; accepted the isolated-loader concern by specifying an absolute rebar.reducer._version import (not a sibling reconciler import) and adding explicit spec-load coverage; accepted child-local documentation validation by adding schema coverage plus make lint to e622; also strengthened e622 export proof to run the full export test file. The absolute-import choice is grounded in inbound_translate already resolving rebar.config/rebar._store/rebar.reducer through absolute package imports while spec-loaded.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan review round 4 disposition (signed PASS, 0 blocking, 4 surfaced advisories): accepted P6 by splitting the compound contract/rollback documentation criterion into independently verifiable ACs; accepted the child regression-guard advisory by adding test_schema_outputs.py to e622 and 568c ACs plus verify metadata; rejected the repeated first-compaction advisory as already remediated by the explicit three-state matrix and unconditional full-downgrade compaction prohibition; rejected a new admin verifier/compactor feature flag as disproportionate scope for this MVP and clarified the safety contract instead: no writer-only binary mode, roll forward for attribution defects, and stop every rebar compact invocation for the entire full-downgrade window. Also accepted the cross-child coaching by pinning _project_legacy_creation_channel(state: dict) -> None and its exact no-op/inferred/unknown behavior, plus function-local absolute import placement in _event_meta.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan review round 5 disposition (signed PASS, 0 blocking): accepted MCP create_identity coverage and threaded mcp through the public identity facade; accepted reducer-level immutability and added a non-CREATE overwrite test; accepted direct Jira-writer validation through the shared runtime validator; accepted runtime show/list/search proof by adding facade projection coverage; grounded the rollback findings against docs/concurrency.md plus compact.rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log and corrected the plan: .retired CREATE sources are permanent, so old-style SNAPSHOT omission is recoverable after re-upgrade. Child 568c now detects/repairs that shape through the existing full-log rebuild seam and tests it. Rejected a true old-binary execution harness as disproportionate for this additive MVP; the test simulates the legacy snapshot shape and proves current recovery. Deferred only low-validity schema-description/ADR suggestions already covered by child 6fe2 and contract docs.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan review round 6 disposition (BLOCK remediated): explicitly require process_edit to skip creation_channel and creation_channel_inferred through a named immutable-field frozenset before generic assignment, and added a parent AC plus child proof. Also aligned the legacy handoff: 6fe2 provisional unknown remains eligible for e622 inference; the helper no-ops only for concrete recorded/already-inferred values and is called explicitly at the end of process_create/process_snapshot. Accepted advisories to split schema/type vs runtime-output ACs, make compaction test filters non-overlapping, assign diagnostic trust-boundary docs to e622 with final integration in 568c, and add gen_types const -> Literal support. The create_core transition is independently deployable because every core caller is updated in 6fe2 while the public Python facade supplies the temporary python default to import until e622 overrides it.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
EVIDENCE for the 'child trailers on origin/main' AC (the completion verifier lacks a git tool, so recording the proof here). The 3-change stack was landed via Gerrit Submit and replicated to origin/main (GitHub navapbc/rebar). `git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q 'rebar-ticket: <id>'` succeeds for all three:
  rebar-ticket: 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41 -> commit dfdac9d63a71 on origin/main
  rebar-ticket: e622-a596-3ceb-4f74 -> commit a7e9058e38f2 on origin/main
  rebar-ticket: 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87 -> commit 4dd9172b9d7a on origin/main (origin/main tip)
Gerrit changes: #764 (6fe2), #765 (e622), #766 (568c) all status=MERGED with LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1. All 3 child stories are status=closed with completion-verifier signatures. The trailer AC is therefore satisfied on origin/main.

compiled_state.description:
# Ticket creation-channel provenance

## Context

Auditors and maintainers can already inspect actor, environment, imported source identifiers, and external bindings, but ticket state does not identify the interface that produced the original CREATE event. That leaves debugging dependent on mutable tags and indirect clues when answering whether a ticket entered through the CLI, MCP server, Python library, Jira reconciler, or NDJSON import.

The current code has two creation seams. Local surfaces converge on src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:create_core through CLI and library/MCP wrappers, while Jira inbound creation composes CREATE data in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound_records.py. Jira-created placeholder identities leave the direct Jira seam through _ensure_inbound_assignee_identity -> rebar.ensure_identity_for -> create_identity_core -> create_core, so that call explicitly overrides the local Python default with jira. Outbound Jira bind/sync operations are not creation events and never rewrite genesis provenance. Existing source_* import provenance in composer.py and reducer/_processors.py is prior art for additive CREATE metadata, but it answers where an imported record came from rather than which local ingress created it.

## Why

Creation channel is a single immutable audit dimension. Recording it on the genesis event gives auditors a direct answer without redefining actor, environment, external ID, assignee, or later mutation provenance. The chosen additive contract fits the event-sourced model, keeps old event logs readable, and avoids unreliable call-stack or tag inference for new writes.

## Architecture Decisions

- Add creation_channel to CREATE data and compiled ticket state with the closed compact vocabulary cli, mcp, python, jira, import, unknown. Define the enum once in src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json and $ref it from TicketState consumers; keep the matching runtime vocabulary plus validator in src/rebar/reducer/_version.py.
- Every live producer declares its channel at its entry boundary. Local create_core callers plus the direct Jira CREATE seam use the shared runtime validator before writing; the core does not inspect the call stack. Direct public Python calls default to python, while MCP create_identity explicitly overrides that default with mcp.
- New writes always record a concrete non-unknown value. Historical records without the field project jira only when all creation-stable signals agree: ticket_id starts with "jira-", author equals LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR (the exact literal "reconciler"), and env_id equals LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID (the exact literal "reconciler"). Define both constants in src/rebar/reducer/_version.py. inbound_translate._event_meta performs the absolute package import (from rebar.reducer._version import LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR, LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID) inside its function body, following the module's established late-import pattern, and the reducer imports the same symbols normally. This does not depend on sibling reconciler package context and is pinned by an isolated spec-load contract test. Missing/None signals or legacy events written under REBAR_AUTHOR/REBAR_ENV_ID overrides intentionally project unknown. The foundation story may provisionally project unknown for a legacy CREATE before Jira inference lands; _project_legacy_creation_channel treats an absent, None, or unknown value without an inference marker as unresolved legacy input and re-evaluates it, but it never touches cli, mcp, python, jira, or import. Successful projections also carry creation_channel_inferred: true.
- The inference marker is present only when true. ticket_state.schema.json encodes that rule formally as {"const": true}; recorded and unknown projections omit the key. It is diagnostic metadata, not a security attestation.
- Import creates a new local ticket through the import channel. Existing source_id, source_created_at, source_author, and source_env continue to describe the source record; an exported source creation_channel is not reused as the new local channel.
- Jira outbound bind/sync events preserve the already-reduced creation_channel and never supply a replacement value; only the inbound materialization CREATE and its placeholder-identity side effect declare jira. In reducer/_processors.py, define _IMMUTABLE_EDIT_FIELDS = frozenset({"creation_channel", "creation_channel_inferred"}) and make process_edit skip those names before its generic state assignment. Specialized processors never assign either field. This named reducer guard makes creation provenance CREATE/SNAPSHOT-only state below individual writers.
- Compaction preserves creation_channel and creation_channel_inferred inside SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state. The SNAPSHOT envelope continues to describe the compactor, not the ticket creator. SNAPSHOT-only replay and snapshot rebuild must reproduce the same creation provenance as full-log replay.
- The public change is additive. Older readers ignore the new CREATE keys; newer readers return unknown for non-Jira legacy records. The MVP deliberately has no writer-only configuration switch or intermediate binary mode; attribution defects are recovered by pausing the affected creation ingress at the deployment boundary and rolling forward. A full downgrade to the immediately pre-feature binary hides the field. Before deploying it, operators stop every scheduled or manual rebar compact invocation and keep compaction/event retirement stopped until the new reader/compactor returns. The rollback matrix is: (a) a new-code SNAPSHOT retains creation_channel when replayed because process_snapshot restores unknown compiled_state keys; (b) an old-binary first compaction omits the field from its SNAPSHOT, but the creation-bearing CREATE remains permanently retained as .retired; (c) a never-compacted ticket retains the value in its active CREATE while the old reader hides it. If the pause is violated, record affected IDs from the downgrade-window compaction activity, re-deploy the new binary, and rebuild those snapshots from the full log with rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log/include_retired; the new SNAPSHOT re-derives provenance from the retained CREATE. Story 568c adds a regression for that compensating-forward recovery. Notify downstream show/list/search/export consumers before any full downgrade.
- No configuration switch or free-form channel extension is added. A future ingress such as a GitHub reconciler extends the closed vocabulary deliberately.

## Scope

IN:
- CREATE writer plumbing for CLI, MCP, Python, Jira inbound, and NDJSON import, plus outbound Jira non-rewrite behavior.
- All CREATE-backed helper flows, including ideas, identities, and session logs, attributed to the interface that invoked them.
- Reducer projection, public ticket-state/output schemas, typed surfaces, export behavior, and documentation.
- Conservative Jira-only historical inference with false-positive controls.
- Compaction, SNAPSHOT-only replay, and snapshot rebuild durability.

OUT:
- Per-mutation provenance, actor/environment/external-ID redesign, cryptographic proof of channel, stack inspection, retroactive event rewrites, analytics dashboards, creation-channel list filters, subchannels for individual commands, and arbitrary custom strings.

## Decomposition and Sequencing

Three child stories deliver independently testable audit increments in dependency order:
1. 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41 — define the contract and expose correct local CLI, MCP, and Python attribution.
2. e622-a596-3ceb-4f74 — add Jira/import attribution and conservative legacy Jira inference; depends on 6fe2.
3. 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87 — prove and preserve recorded, inferred, and unknown values through compaction and SNAPSHOT replay/rebuild; depends on e622.

The stories share reducer surfaces and therefore run sequentially through depends_on links. This is a multi-story feature and should use a Gerrit feature branch named feature/creation-channel-provenance; each child lands there through both review votes, followed by one gated no-ff merge change to main.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Missed producer path: enumerate every create_core and direct CREATE writer call site and pin each public facade/helper with a behavioral test.
- False historical attribution: require the exact three-signal Jira predicate and add near-miss negative cases; never infer CLI, MCP, Python, or import.
- Snapshot omission/recovery: assert the fields inside compiled_state after source CREATE retirement, keep .retired CREATE sources as the recovery oracle, and test rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log after a simulated old-style SNAPSHOT omits provenance.
- Contract drift: define the enum once, validate at writer boundaries, and reference the same vocabulary from the JSON schema and documentation.
- Compatibility: keep the change additive and optional on stored historical events; no migration or destructive rewrite is performed.
- Downgrade first-compaction omission: the MVP has no writer-only binary mode. Roll forward from attribution defects; before a full binary downgrade, stop every rebar compact invocation. If that pause is violated, re-upgrade and rebuild affected snapshots from permanently retained .retired CREATE sources. Document and test the three-state matrix plus this recovery.

## Success Criteria

- Auditors can distinguish cli, mcp, python, jira, and import creation through existing show/list/export state projections.
- Historical Jira records are labeled jira with creation_channel_inferred=true only when the deterministic predicate matches; all other legacy records report unknown.
- Every CREATE-backed ticket/entity helper reports the invoking interface rather than its internal implementation path.
- Compaction and snapshot rebuild preserve the exact channel and inference marker after original events are retired.
- The public schema and documentation define the vocabulary, default/fallback semantics, import behavior, and diagnostic trust boundary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CLI, MCP, plus direct Python creation report cli, mcp, plus python respectively across standard tickets, ideas, identities, plus session logs. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k "cli or mcp or python".
- [ ] Newly materialized Jira issues plus reconciler-created placeholder identities report jira. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k inbound_recorded.
- [ ] Outbound Jira binding or synchronization preserves the existing local creation channel. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k outbound.
- [ ] process_edit plus every specialized mutation processor refuse to overwrite creation_channel or creation_channel_inferred after genesis. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k immutable.
- [ ] NDJSON import reports import while retaining source_* record provenance. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/io/test_creation_channel.py -k import.
- [ ] Legacy Jira inference fires only for the exact three-signal predicate; every near miss reports unknown without creation_channel_inferred. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k legacy.
- [ ] TicketState JSON schemas plus generated types expose the six-value enum with an optional Literal[True] inference marker. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_ticket_state.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_outputs.py && python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types --check.
- [ ] Runtime show/list/search projections plus exported NDJSON expose creation_channel and the inference marker when present. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/facades/test_creation_channel.py -k projections tests/interfaces/io/test_export_ndjson.py.
- [ ] Recorded or inferred values survive normal compaction after folded CREATE retirement; a simulated old-style SNAPSHOT omission is recoverable from the retained CREATE through full-log rebuild. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k "recorded or inferred or rollback_recovery".
- [ ] Legacy-SNAPSHOT fallback, SNAPSHOT-only replay, partial-horizon compaction, plus snapshot rebuild return the specified recorded/inferred/unknown projection. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_creation_channel_compaction.py -k "legacy_snapshot or snapshot_only or horizon or rebuild" tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_snapshot.py.
- [ ] Every child story reports status closed in the shared store. Prove with uv run rebar show 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41 | jq -e '.status == "closed"' && uv run rebar show e622-a596-3ceb-4f74 | jq -e '.status == "closed"' && uv run rebar show 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87 | jq -e '.status == "closed"'.
- [ ] Each child ticket trailer is present on origin/main after the gated feature-branch merge. Prove with git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41" && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: e622-a596-3ceb-4f74" && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87".
- [ ] docs/event-schema.md plus docs/output-schemas.md define CREATE/SNAPSHOT placement, the enum, Jira inference, import semantics, the distinction from actor/environment/source_* provenance, plus the diagnostic-only trust boundary: creation_channel_inferred is not a security attestation. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py && make lint.
- [ ] docs/concurrency.md defines the full-downgrade three-state schema matrix, the unconditional compaction pause, the affected-ID capture step, plus compensating-forward rebuild from retained .retired CREATE files. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py && make lint.

file_impact.reason: local create entry boundaries and shared composer
public enum and TicketState output contract
event, output, import, and compaction contract documentation

compiled_state.title: Track immutable ticket creation channel across every ingress path

verify_commands.dd_text: All ingress channels and legacy inference are behaviorally correct
Public schemas and generated types expose the stable additive contract
Creation provenance survives compaction and snapshot replay/rebuild
